title: Gurania capitata

intro: Gurania capitata, also known as the "cup gurania," is a tropical plant species belonging to the Cucurbitaceae family. Traditionally, Gurania capitata has been used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Gurania capitata is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
